title: Gently Braised Middle-Eastern Lamb Loin Chops Tagine
description: A rich, creamy tagine dish featuring tender lamb loin chops and a blend of Middle-Eastern spices. Perfect for meal prep or a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Season the lamb loin chops with salt, black pepper, cumin, coriander, and paprika.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb loin chops until browned on both sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the lamb loin chops to a mini cocotte or ramekin.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger; cook for 1 minute.
Add the chicken broth to the skillet and bring to a simmer.
Pour the chicken broth mixture over the lamb loin chops in the mini cocotte.
Cover the mini cocotte with a lid or foil and braise in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes.
Remove the mini cocotte from the oven and stir in the heavy cream.
Return the mini cocotte to the oven and braise for an additional 5-7 minutes, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
